note: Proposals for human review. Nothing here is applied to roadmap.json automatically. Promote approved items by hand.

Robust LMS rebuild is paused; priority is creating and storing course packets first, then building a simpler MVP display later. work_item_candidates.suggestion: Crew Blueprint Course and Safety Positioning
work_item_candidates.detail: Status: active Project: Crew Blueprint training content Decision: Crew Blueprint is a job-readiness training platform for stagehands/live-event workers. Course 1 is Stagehand Fundamentals, with modules, quizzes, 80% pass threshold, retakes, and a canonical lesson structure. The platform must avoid unauthorized certification claims and consistently position training as orientation/job-readiness unless certification is actually authorized. Pillar: pillar-2 (The Crew Blueprint) Evidence: `memories/

Status: active Project: Crew Blueprint brand/design Decision: Approved brand direction is dark industrial, with charcoal/near-black surfaces, industrial gold/amber accents, off-white text, muted gray secondary text, mobile-first layout, and avoidance of generic school, white-page, neon, gaming, cluttered, or tiny-text designs. Pillar: pillar-2 (The Crew Blueprint) Evidence: `memories/processed/chunks/chatgpt-export-2026-07-05/chatgpt-export-2026-07-05__aaron_bowman_context_export_md__04_THE_CREW

Status: Critical priority (blocks Deadhang site linking) Decision: Crew Blueprint must reach MVP state 100% before cross-linking Pillar: pillar-2 (The Crew Blueprint) Evidence: User explicitly deprioritized Deadhang site work until Blueprint MVP complete ---

work_item_candidates.suggestion: Roadmap DNA vs. Crew Blueprint Toolkit Boundary
work_item_candidates.detail: Status: active Project: 50-year roadmap / The Crew Blueprint boundary Decision: Frequency, principles, Watch/Do/Teach philosophy, and deeper why belong in the 50-year roadmap repository, not inside The Crew Blueprint curriculum. The Crew Blueprint should remain a professional technical education hub. Pillar: pillar-2 (The Crew Blueprint) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`.

Status: active Project: The Crew Blueprint Decision: The Crew Blueprint should focus on stagehand, rigging, AV, and live-event production industry materials. It should not become the full Homes for Hands philosophy vault or a broad general-lifestyle platform. Pillar: pillar-2 (The Crew Blueprint) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`.

Status: active Project: The Crew Blueprint repository structure Decision: The Crew Blueprint should include granular pathways for specific jobs in the field, not only broad categories. Stagehand/Ground Labor must be represented as its own discipline because ground labor is a large share of the workforce. Pillar: pillar-2 (The Crew Blueprint) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`. ---

blocker_candidates.detail: Claude’s source URL pass was interrupted. The app needs small-batch official source verification before broader cleanup or expansion.

blocker_candidates.detail: Prior audit found a helper-script approach that may violate owner-file rules. Long-term fix should canonicalize cycle behavior in owner files/data rather than stacking workaround scripts.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Production Atlas public research has verification gaps
blocker_candidates.detail: Public crew logistics data is thin; worker support, labor routes, and non-audio vendor stacks frequently require structured unknown fields and human verification.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Production Atlas validation cannot be confirmed in connector-only environments
blocker_candidates.detail: `npm run validate:all` is required for app data changes, but validation must be run in a real workspace or GitHub Actions environment before claiming clean status.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Accommodation and travel intelligence remains largely unfilled
blocker_candidates.detail: active records still need accommodation, travel, and per-diem research, with top-scoring records prioritized. Unknown must stay unknown when public evidence is not available.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Schedule remains off public header navigation
blocker_candidates.detail: Schedule exists by direct URL but should not return to primary navigation until mobile usability issues are resolved.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Production Atlas research cannot rely on public sources alone for crew logistics
blocker_candidates.detail: Public data is too thin for worker support, labor routes, full vendor stack, and local-jurisdiction confirmation; continuing broad research creates many unknown fields.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Production Atlas needs a human-verification assistance mechanism
blocker_candidates.detail: The user stated they would not gather human verification without assistance; the system needs a framework/tool for assisted verification before non-public logistics can be completed safely.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Production-focused festival feed needs source-quality gates
blocker_candidates.detail: The RSS/intelligence feed must avoid becoming a generic festival directory or noisy job aggregator. It needs production-intent filtering, source provenance, deduplication, manual approval for borderline items, and RSS/feed validation tests.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Public vendor staffing relationships remain mostly unverified
blocker_candidates.detail: The app must not present employer leads as confirmed festival-specific vendors without source-backed proof.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: 2026 active status source URLs are incomplete
blocker_candidates.detail: Some active statuses came from a user-provided report without direct source URLs; those fields still need official/public source-link attachment.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Data structure still needs continued normalization and UI work
blocker_candidates.detail: Later phases should add better card displays for lodging, travel, per diem, opportunity type, value score, missing-data checklists, and verification filters.

blocker_candidates.detail: Calendar sync/event creation needs automatic renewal and persistence to avoid manual refresh steps and failed scheduled jobs.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Laravel/back-end work risks overbuilding before validation
blocker_candidates.detail: Laravel is the right long-term backbone, but the first phase must stay narrow: auth, roles, permissions, admin space, and core data models.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Invoice parser/PDF architecture must not drift
blocker_candidates.detail: The invoice tool should stay narrow and deterministic. Risks include incorrect OpenRouter usage, confusing HTML-to-PDF with pdfmake, using too many pages, adding storage too early, using absolute positioning for variable rows, or relying on browser print as the final PDF method.

work_item_candidates.suggestion: Homes for Hands Nonprofit Respite Project
work_item_candidates.detail: Status: active Project: Homes for Hands / Homestead Network nonprofit layer Decision: Homes for Hands should be tracked as a nonprofit homestead project for stagehands and travelers who need respite along the road between gigs. It should also preserve the possibility of a permanent space for people who need a stable landing point rather than only short-term rest. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network) Evidence: User clarification on 2026-07-06 during roadmap cleanup conversation.

Status: active Project: Homestead Network identity Decision: The homestead/social enterprise is not only housing. It is framed as a refuge and rebuilding environment for displaced or unconventional people who can live with integrity, contribute, learn, and protect the culture of the place. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network) Evidence: `memories/processed/chunks/Gemini_Building_Profitable_Social_Enterprises_-_2026-07-06_01-07-46/Gemini_Building_Profitable_Social_Enterprises_-_2026-07-06_01-07-46

Status: active Project: Homestead governance model Decision: Day-to-day property authority should belong to the property steward rather than Aaron directly. Aaron’s role is to select stewards with sound judgment. The steward should preserve respect, order, and culture without unnecessary micromanagement. Status: active Project: Homes for Hands / Homestead Network Decision: Homes for Hands should function as a welcoming respite space for travelers and people needing rest, whether or not they are stagehands. Stagehands remain the first and strongest service population, but the long-term vision should not permanently exclude other travelers needing respite. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network / Homes for Hands) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-

Status: active Project: Homes for Hands service model Decision: The roadmap should distinguish between transient guests, recurring community members, inner-core permanent residents, and stewards. Most people will pass through temporarily; permanent residents are self-selected by desire, alignment, sincerity, and willingness to carry the culture. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network / Homes for Hands) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-cont

Status: active Project: Homes for Hands operating culture Decision: Space care should be part of the culture. Guests and residents should leave spaces as they found them. The model may include RVs, tents, utilities, simple indoor space use, nominal fees, and low-friction turnover rather than a heavy hotel-style room turnover operation. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network / Homes for Hands) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`.

work_item_candidates.suggestion: Homes for Hands Teach-First Boundary Model
work_item_candidates.detail: Status: active Project: Homes for Hands governance Decision: Violations of the space should first be handled as opportunities to teach the why behind the community standard. If disruption is destructive and the person is not sincere in repairing the harm, they are no longer welcome unless they sincerely make it right. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network / Homes for Hands) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`.

work_item_candidates.suggestion: Homes for Hands Guardrails Over Forced Belief
work_item_candidates.detail: Status: active Project: Homes for Hands governance and culture Decision: The project should not attempt to force belief, frequency, or worldview into others. It should create guardrails that guide truth, care, responsibility, and Watch/Do/Teach practice without turning the culture into a rigid manual. Pillar: pillar-6 (Homestead Network / Homes for Hands) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`.

Status: active Project: Deadhang Labor LLC / Homes for Hands interface Decision: Jobsite conduct can be one fluid and intuitive signal for whether someone may align with deeper community participation. It should remain one variable, not a rigid residency gate. Pillar: pillar-1 (Deadhang Labor LLC) Evidence: `data/roadmap-deep-context/2026-07-06-gemini-tribe-fit-truth-vs-transgression-deep-context.md`.

blocker_candidates.detail: The nonprofit concept needs an operational model for respite stays, eligibility, traveler/stagehand intake, length-of-stay expectations, permanent-space criteria, stewardship responsibilities, and boundaries between temporary rest and long-term placement.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Homes for Hands service model still needs operating definitions
blocker_candidates.detail: Respite stays, RV/tent/utility use, nominal fees, indoor vs. outdoor stay options, turnover expectations, resident categories, steward authority, and re-entry after harm need structured definitions.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Homes for Hands stewardship and selection model remains unresolved
blocker_candidates.detail: The roadmap must define how stewards protect the space without becoming rigid gatekeepers, micromanagers, or overburdened maintainers.

blocker_candidates.suggestion: Roadmap time-layer needs concrete near-term milestones
blocker_candidates.detail: The 50-year roadmap needs near-term milestone planning before more long-range Homes for Hands planning.

unmapped_claims.title: Producer/vendor evidence often lacks URL-level extraction
Code review workflows need actual payload enforcement
